prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the year of our Lord two thousand and twenty-three, the guild known as SaaS hath revealed its scrolls of numbers in the third phase of the sun's journey. Verily, their coffers overflowed with coin aplenty, as more than a thousand knights and squires did pledge their fealty through the ethereal realm of the web. Despite a dragon’s roar of competition, the clattering keystrokes of scribes dispatched their digital missives far and wide, reaching hearts as a bard's tune upon the wind. The lords of this mystical realm did boast of artifacts most strange, named "software as a service," which, like a wizard's spell, did multiply their treasures whilst resting not upon the ground, saving many a laborer's back.
